1: In the seventh month month , in the one and twentieth day day of the month, came the word of the LORD LORD by the prophet Haggai, saying,
2: Speak now to Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua the son of Josedech, the high priest, and to the residue of the people, saying,
3: Who is is left among you that saw this house in her first glory? and how do ye see it now? is it is it not in your eyes in comparison of it as nothing?
4: Yet now be strong, O Zerubbabel, saith the LORD LORD ; and be strong, O Joshua, son of Josedech, the high priest; and be strong, all ye people of the land, saith the LORD LORD , and work: for I am am with you, saith the LORD LORD of hosts:
5: According to According to the word that I covenanted with you when ye came out of Egypt, so my spirit remaineth among you: fear ye not.
6: For thus saith the LORD LORD of hosts; Yet once, it is is a little while, and I will shake the heavens, and the earth, and the sea, and the dry land land ;
7: and I will shake all nations, and the desire of all nations shall come: and I will fill this house with glory, saith the LORD LORD of hosts.
8: The silver is is mine, and the gold is is mine, saith the LORD LORD of hosts.
9: The glory of this latter house shall be greater than of the former, saith the LORD LORD of hosts: and in this place will I give peace, saith the LORD LORD of hosts.
10: In the four and twentieth day day of the ninth month month , in the second year of Darius, came the word of the LORD LORD by Haggai the prophet, saying,
11: Thus saith the LORD LORD of hosts; Ask now the priests concerning concerning the law, saying,
12: If one bear holy flesh in the skirt of his garment, and with his skirt do touch bread, or pottage, or wine, or oil, or any meat, shall it be holy? And the priests answered and said, No.
13: Then said Haggai, If one that is one that is unclean by a dead body touch any of these, shall it be unclean? And the priests answered and said, It shall be unclean.
14: Then answered Haggai, and said, So is is this people, and so is is this nation before me, saith the LORD LORD ; and so is is every work of their hands; and that which they offer there is is unclean.
15: And now, I pray you, consider from this day and upward, from before a stone was laid upon a stone in the temple of the LORD LORD :
16: since those days days were, when one one came to an heap of twenty measures measures , there were but but ten: when one one came to the pressfat for to draw out fifty vessels vessels out of the press, there were but but twenty.
17: I smote you with blasting and with mildew and with hail in all the labours of your hands; yet ye turned turned not to me, saith the LORD LORD .
18: Consider now from this day and upward, from the four and twentieth day of the ninth month, even month, even from the day that the foundation of the LORD LORD 's temple was laid, consider it it .
19: Is the seed yet in the barn? yea, as yet the vine, and the fig tree, and the pomegranate, and the olive tree, hath not brought forth: from this day will I bless you you .
20: And again the word of the LORD LORD came unto Haggai in the four and twentieth day day of the month, saying,
21: Speak to Zerubbabel, governor of Judah, saying, I will shake the heavens and the earth;
22: and I will overthrow the throne of kingdoms, and I will destroy the strength of the kingdoms of the heathen; and I will overthrow the chariots, and those that ride in them; and the horses and their riders shall come down, every one by the sword of his brother.
23: In that day, saith the LORD LORD of hosts, will I take thee, O Zerubbabel, my servant, the son of Shealtiel, saith the LORD LORD , and will make thee as a signet: for I have chosen thee, saith the LORD LORD of hosts.
